title: milium vulgare
intro: Milium vulgare, also known as wild garlic or bear's garlic, is a perennial plant belonging to the Allium family, characterized by its strong onion-like scent and edible flowers. Traditionally, Milium vulgare has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits also include its potential to lower cholesterol levels and reduce the risk of heart disease. Milium vulgare can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in cooking and as a natural pest deterrent.
